questions.explanation: "yesterday" signals the past simple. "go" is an irregular verb: go → went.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We ___ football when it started to rain."
questions.explanation: The football game was already in progress (background action) when the rain interrupted. Use past continuous: were playing.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct negative: "He ___ the test last Monday."
questions.explanation: Past simple negative: didn't + base verb. "last Monday" confirms past simple, not perfect.
"ever" is used in present perfect questions to ask about life experience: Have you ever eaten...?

questions.prompt: Complete: "I ___ my homework. I can play now."
questions.explanation: The action is very recent and its result matters now (I can play). Use present perfect with "just": have just finished.

options.text: Did they go to Djerba last summer?
Did they went to Djerba last summer?
Have they gone to Djerba last summer?
Were they go to Djerba last summer?

questions.explanation: "last summer" is a specific past time, so use past simple. Questions: Did + subject + base verb. Never add -ed/-en after did.
